name: Potential Privilege Escalation via Instance Profile (AWS)
notes: This rule triggers when an admin action is performed after AWS instance profile has changed. A user may change AWS instance profile to assign a different role. If an admin activity follows after such activity, there is a potential this is a privilege escalation event.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regular Virtualization Administration match at least 1 times with the same Profile (custom) in 1 hour(s) after BB:BehaviourDefinition: Modification in Instance Profile (AWS) match
conditions_text: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regular Virtualization Administration match at least 1 times with the same Profile (custom) in 1 hour(s) after BB:BehaviourDefinition: Modification in Instance Profile (AWS) match
thresholds: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regular Virtualization Administration match at least 1 times with the same Profile (custom) in 1 hour(s) after BB:BehaviourDefinition: Modification in Instance Profile (AWS) match
TD_list: APPLY Potential Privilege Escalation via Instance Profile (AWS) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regular Virtualization Administration match at least 1 times with the same Profile (custom) in 1 hour(s) after BB:BehaviourDefinition: Modification in Instance Profile (AWS) match
RED_list: Event name: Potential Privilege Escalation via Instance Profile (AWS)
Event description: Detects when an admin action is performed after AWS instance profile has changed.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Profile (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
